---
title: "How LangChain & LangGraph Work, Visually"
subtitle: "One coffee shop, two libraries: prompts, chains, tools, memory, graphs, checkpoints, interrupts, and the ten questions that decide production"
author: "Pranjal Rawat"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: black
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    incremental: false
    slide-number: c/t
    controls: true
    progress: true
    width: 1200
    height: 720
    margin: 0.055
    center: true
    navigation-mode: linear
---

```{=html}
<style>
:root {
  --bg: #16171a; --fg: #e6e6e9; --muted: #8b8b92; --sub: #a3a3ab;
  --accent: #7cb0de; --feat: #6bbf8a; --amber: #d9a441; --rule: #2a2c31;
  --node: #1d2733; --edge: #5a6472; --ink: #0f1114; --pink: #e06b9c;
  --violet: #b19cf5;
}
.reveal { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; }
.reveal, .reveal .slides section { background: var(--bg); color: var(--fg); }
.reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3 { color: var(--fg); font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: -0.5px; text-transform: none; }
.reveal h2 { font-size: 1.55em; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--rule); padding-bottom: 0.25em; margin-bottom: 0.5em; }
.reveal a { color: var(--accent); }
.reveal .slide-number { background: none; color: var(--muted); }
.reveal .footer, .reveal .slide-number { font-size: 12px; }
.reveal strong { color: var(--accent); }
.reveal em { color: var(--sub); font-style: italic; }
.reveal .subtle { color: var(--sub); font-size: 0.8em; }
.reveal .src { color: var(--muted); font-size: 0.55em; margin-top: 0.8em; }
.reveal .lead { font-size: 1.15em; line-height: 1.5; }
.reveal code { color: var(--feat); background: rgba(124,176,222,0.08); padding: 1px 6px; border-radius: 4px; }
.reveal section img { background: none; border: none; box-shadow: none; }
/* SVG shared visual language */
.fig { width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 62vh; display: block; margin: 0 auto; }
.node { fill: var(--node); stroke: var(--accent); stroke-width: 2.5; }
.node.feat { stroke: var(--feat); }
.node.hot { fill: #3a2f14; stroke: var(--amber); }
.node.ghost { fill: #191c20; stroke: var(--edge); }
.node.bad { stroke: var(--pink); }
.cap { fill: var(--fg); font: 16px -apple-system, sans-serif; text-anchor: middle; }
.cap.big { font-size: 21px; font-weight: 600; }
.zonelabel { fill: var(--muted); font: 600 14px -apple-system, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1.5px; text-anchor: middle; }
.edge { stroke: var(--edge); stroke-width: 2.5; fill: none; marker-end: url(#arrow); }
.flow { stroke: var(--accent); stroke-width: 2.5; fill: none; marker-end: url(#arrowA); stroke-dasharray: 6 5; }
.zone { fill: none; stroke: var(--rule); stroke-width: 1.5; rx: 12; }
.card { fill: var(--node); stroke: var(--rule); stroke-width: 1.5; rx: 8; }
.card.hot { stroke: var(--amber); }
.card.ok { stroke: var(--feat); }
.card.bad { stroke: var(--pink); }
.cardtext { fill: var(--fg); font: 15px ui-monospace, monospace; }
.note { fill: var(--sub); font: 15px -apple-system, sans-serif; }
.reveal .fragment.grow-in { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.9); transition: all 0.3s ease; }
.reveal .fragment.grow-in.visible { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); }
/* draw-on edges */
.edge.draw, .flow.draw { stroke-dasharray: 1; stroke-dashoffset: 1; }
.reveal .fragment.visible .edge.draw,
.reveal .fragment.visible .flow.draw,
.reveal .edge.draw.fragment.visible,
.reveal .flow.draw.fragment.visible { animation: gv-draw 0.55s ease both; }
@keyframes gv-draw { from { stroke-dashoffset: 1; } to { stroke-dashoffset: 0; } }
.reveal .fragment.pop { opacity: 0; transform-box: fill-box; transform-origin: center; }
.reveal .fragment.pop.visible { animation: gv-pop 0.45s cubic-bezier(0.2,0.8,0.2,1) both; }
@keyframes gv-pop { 0% { opacity: 0; transform: scale(0.5); } 60% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1.06); } 100% { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1); } }
</style>

<svg width="0" height="0" style="position:absolute" aria-hidden="true">
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8.5" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z" fill="#5a6472"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arrowA" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="8.5" refY="5" markerWidth="7" markerHeight="7" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z" fill="#7cb0de"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>
</svg>
```

# Two libraries, one idea {.center}

::: lead
A model call is a pure function: messages in, message out. Everything an
application needs beyond that one call is plumbing, and every team was
rebuilding the same plumbing by hand.
:::

**LangChain** standardizes one call: prompts, parsing, tools, memory.
**LangGraph** choreographs many calls: state, branching, persistence, interruption.

::: notes
The deck is one long argument. Part 1 shows what one composed call buys
you. Part 2 shows why real conversations need cycles and durable state.
Part 3 asks the questions that decide whether any of it survives
production. The sibling decks cover git, RAG, and agentic harnesses; this
one covers the two libraries most teams reach for first.
:::

---

## Meet Beanline Coffee

Every figure ahead is a photograph of real code running one small coffee shop: a real menu, a real stock room with counts that go down, a real till with a balance that moves, and a barista bot built up from raw calls to a full graph.

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 400">
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="card" x="60" y="120" width="250" height="90"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="82" y="152">scripted model</text>
    <text class="note" x="82" y="182" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">a fixed list of replies,</text>
    <text class="note" x="82" y="200" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">so every run replays exactly</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="node" x="380" y="100" width="240" height="130" rx="12"/>
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="150">Beanline's bot</text>
    <text class="note" x="500" y="185" text-anchor="middle" style="font-size:13px">real langchain + langgraph:</text>
    <text class="note" x="500" y="203" text-anchor="middle" style="font-size:13px">prompts, tools, graphs, checkpoints</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="3">
    <rect class="card ok" x="690" y="90" width="250" height="150"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="712" y="122">the shop</text>
    <text class="note" x="712" y="150" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">menu: latte $4.50, sizes, extras</text>
    <text class="note" x="712" y="172" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">stock room: oat milk, 2 left</text>
    <text class="note" x="712" y="194" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">till: $200.00 and counting</text>
    <text class="note" x="712" y="216" text-anchor="start" style="font-size:13px">all mutable, all asserted</text>
  </g>
  <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 310 165 L 375 165"/>
  <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="3" pathLength="1" d="M 620 165 L 685 165"/>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="4">
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="320">The model's words are scripted. Everything else executes.</text>
    <text class="note" x="500" y="355" text-anchor="middle">Prompts really render, parsers really raise, tools really check stock, graphs really pause and resume. Nothing is a mockup.</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

::: notes
Same honesty contract as the sibling decks. The one fake is the model's
dialogue, played by langchain's own GenericFakeChatModel through the real
code paths, which is exactly what makes every figure assertable by code.
Watch for one recurring number: a large oat-milk latte is $6.25.
:::

---

## Ten questions before you ship an agent

The APIs are the easy part. Practitioners converge on the same checklist, and the four in amber are the ones teams skip.

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 480">
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="card" x="40" y="50" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="75">1. Does this need an agent at all?</text>
    <text class="note" x="60" y="97" style="font-size:12px">or a pipeline, a classifier, one structured call</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="40" y="125" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="150" style="fill:var(--amber)">2. What exactly counts as success?</text>
    <text class="note" x="60" y="172" style="font-size:12px">the reservation exists, not "sounds booked"</text>
    <rect class="card" x="40" y="200" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="225">3. What can it see and do?</text>
    <text class="note" x="60" y="247" style="font-size:12px">tools with typed args, real evidence back</text>
    <rect class="card" x="40" y="275" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="300">4. Which decisions stay deterministic?</text>
    <text class="note" x="60" y="322" style="font-size:12px">the model routes; the till does arithmetic</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="40" y="350" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="375" style="fill:var(--amber)">5. What is the authoritative state?</text>
    <text class="note" x="60" y="397" style="font-size:12px">a schema you can draw, not a transcript</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="card" x="520" y="50" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="540" y="75">6. What is the simplest graph that works?</text>
    <text class="note" x="540" y="97" style="font-size:12px">every extra node must earn its keep</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="520" y="125" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="540" y="150" style="fill:var(--amber)">7. When does it stop, retry, escalate?</text>
    <text class="note" x="540" y="172" style="font-size:12px">bounded loops, policies per failure class</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="520" y="200" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="540" y="225" style="fill:var(--amber)">8. Can it resume without double-charging?</text>
    <text class="note" x="540" y="247" style="font-size:12px">replayed nodes must not repeat side effects</text>
    <rect class="card" x="520" y="275" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="540" y="300">9. What authority does it have?</text>
    <text class="note" x="540" y="322" style="font-size:12px">high-blast-radius actions wait for a human</text>
    <rect class="card" x="520" y="350" width="440" height="60"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="540" y="375">10. What does each model call buy?</text>
    <text class="note" x="540" y="397" style="font-size:12px">quality vs cost, measured call by call</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="3">
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="455">This deck answers all ten, with running code</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

::: {.src}
Synthesis: OpenAI, A practical guide to building agents · Anthropic, Building effective agents · Anthropic, Demystifying evals · LangChain persistence & interrupt docs · AutoGen termination docs
:::

::: notes
The list is the consensus of the major agent-engineering guides, reordered
by where teams actually get hurt. Questions 2, 5, 7 and 8 are the
underestimated four, and each gets its own chapter in Part 3.
:::

---

<!--FB:the-problem|Gluing Model Calls by Hand-->

---

# Part 1 · LangChain: one call, composed {.center}

::: lead
Everything in Part 1 is about a single model call and the plumbing around
it: what goes in, what comes out, and who remembers.
:::

---

<!--FB:messages-not-strings|The Conversation Is a Typed List-->

---

<!--FB:prompts-are-functions|Prompts Are Functions-->

---

## The pipe changes the payload's type

One order, four shapes. LCEL's `|` is function composition, and the thing flowing through really changes type at every hop.

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 400">
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="card" x="40" y="150" width="190" height="90"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="182">{"name": "Maya",</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="60" y="206"> "input": "..."}</text>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="135" y="130">DICT</text>
  </g>
  <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 230 195 L 285 195"/>
  <text class="note fragment" data-fragment-index="2" x="257" y="175" text-anchor="middle" style="font-size:13px">prompt</text>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="card" x="290" y="150" width="190" height="90"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="310" y="182">[system, human]</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="310" y="206">rendered, typed</text>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="385" y="130">CHATPROMPTVALUE</text>
  </g>
  <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="3" pathLength="1" d="M 480 195 L 535 195"/>
  <text class="note fragment" data-fragment-index="3" x="507" y="175" text-anchor="middle" style="font-size:13px">model</text>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="3">
    <rect class="card" x="540" y="150" width="190" height="90"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="560" y="182">role: ai</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="560" y="206">+ tool_calls, meta</text>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="635" y="130">AIMESSAGE</text>
  </g>
  <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="4" pathLength="1" d="M 730 195 L 785 195"/>
  <text class="note fragment" data-fragment-index="4" x="757" y="175" text-anchor="middle" style="font-size:13px">parser</text>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="4">
    <rect class="card ok" x="790" y="150" width="170" height="90"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="810" y="182">"One mocha,</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="810" y="206"> $5.00"</text>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="875" y="130">STR</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="5">
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="330">prompt | model | parser</text>
    <text class="note" x="500" y="365" text-anchor="middle">Composable because uniform: every stage answers .invoke, .batch and .stream</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

::: notes
This is the one static diagram Part 1 leans on; the flipbook that follows
shows the same pipe with the real payloads photographed at each hop.
:::

---

<!--FB:chains-are-pipes|Chains Are Pipes-->

---

<!--FB:from-prose-to-objects|From Prose to Objects-->

---

<!--FB:tools-are-round-trips|Tools Are Round Trips-->

---

<!--FB:memory-is-yours|Memory Is Yours, Not the Model's-->

---

<!--FB:streaming|The Same Answer, Sooner-->

---

# Part 2 · LangGraph: many calls, choreographed {.center}

::: lead
A pipe points one way. A real conversation loops, branches, pauses, and
survives a restart. LangGraph's move is to make your control flow an
object: nodes, edges, and state you can draw, checkpoint, and interrupt.
:::

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 300">
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="node ghost" x="70" y="110" width="120" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <rect class="node ghost" x="240" y="110" width="120" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <rect class="node ghost" x="410" y="110" width="120" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <path class="edge" d="M 190 137 L 235 137"/>
    <path class="edge" d="M 360 137 L 405 137"/>
    <text class="note" x="300" y="220" text-anchor="middle">a chain: one direction, no memory of place</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="node" x="640" y="50" width="130" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="705" y="83" style="font-size:14px">take_order</text>
    <rect class="node" x="820" y="50" width="130" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="885" y="83" style="font-size:14px">check_stock</text>
    <rect class="node feat" x="820" y="180" width="130" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="885" y="213" style="font-size:14px">confirm</text>
    <rect class="node hot" x="640" y="180" width="130" height="55" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="705" y="213" style="font-size:14px">suggest_alt</text>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 770 77 L 815 77"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 885 105 L 885 175"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="3" pathLength="1" d="M 815 207 L 775 207"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="3" pathLength="1" d="M 705 175 L 705 110"/>
    <text class="note fragment" data-fragment-index="3" x="795" y="270" text-anchor="middle" style="fill:var(--amber)">a graph: the back-edge is a real, drawable edge</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

---

<!--FB:chains-cant-loop|Chains Can't Loop-->

---

<!--FB:state-and-reducers|State and Reducers-->

---

<!--FB:graphs-are-data|Graphs Are Data-->

---

<!--FB:conditional-edges|Branching on Real State-->

---

## ReAct, in five lines

The famous agent loop is small enough to memorize, and LangGraph ships it as a two-node graph you have already built by hand.

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 380">
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="zone" x="60" y="50" width="420" height="280"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="270" y="80">THE PSEUDOCODE</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="95" y="125">while True:</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="95" y="155">    reply = model(messages)</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="95" y="185">    if not reply.tool_calls:</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="95" y="215">        return reply</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="95" y="245">    messages += run(reply.tool_calls)</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="zone" x="540" y="50" width="400" height="280"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="740" y="80">THE GRAPH</text>
    <rect class="node" x="620" y="110" width="110" height="60" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="675" y="145">model</text>
    <rect class="node" x="800" y="110" width="110" height="60" rx="10"/>
    <text class="cap" x="855" y="145">tools</text>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 730 125 C 760 110, 770 110, 795 125"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="2" pathLength="1" d="M 795 155 C 770 170, 760 170, 730 155"/>
    <path class="flow draw fragment" data-fragment-index="3" pathLength="1" d="M 675 170 L 675 260"/>
    <text class="note fragment" data-fragment-index="3" x="675" y="285" text-anchor="middle">no tool_calls: END</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="4">
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="360">create_agent(model, tools) is this exact shape</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

::: notes
The point of showing the pseudocode first is that the prebuilt is not
magic. The conditional edge is "did the model ask for a tool," and the
cycle is the while loop made drawable.
:::

---

<!--FB:the-react-agent|The Agent Is a Two-Node Cycle-->

---

<!--FB:threads-and-checkpoints|Walk Away, Come Back-->

---

# Part 3 · The questions that decide production {.center}

::: lead
Everything so far was mechanism. What separates a demo from a system is a
set of decisions, and four of them are chronically skipped: what counts as
success, where state lives, when to stop, and what happens on replay.
:::

::: {.src}
OpenAI, A practical guide to building agents · Anthropic, Demystifying evals for AI agents · LangChain interrupt docs
:::

---

<!--FB:do-you-need-an-agent|Do You Need an Agent at All?-->

---

<!--FB:define-done|Define Done, Verifiably-->

---

<!--FB:stop-retry-escalate|Stop, Retry, Escalate-->

---

<!--FB:approve-before-act|Approve Before You Act-->

---

<!--FB:resume-without-double-charge|Resume Without Double-Charging-->

---

# The Whole Shop {.center}

---

## Every abstraction, one order

Maya's large oat-milk latte, one last time, touching every piece the deck introduced.

```{=html}
<svg class="fig" viewBox="0 0 1000 500">
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="1">
    <rect class="zone" x="40" y="50" width="280" height="200"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="180" y="78">ONE CALL (LANGCHAIN)</text>
    <rect class="card" x="65" y="95" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="85" y="120" style="font-size:13px">prompt: menu + {input}</text>
    <rect class="card" x="65" y="145" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="85" y="170" style="font-size:13px">model -> Order object</text>
    <rect class="card" x="65" y="195" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="85" y="220" style="font-size:13px">tools: check_stock, price</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="2">
    <rect class="zone" x="360" y="50" width="280" height="200"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="500" y="78">MANY CALLS (LANGGRAPH)</text>
    <rect class="node" x="385" y="95" width="100" height="45" rx="8"/>
    <text class="cap" x="435" y="123" style="font-size:12px">take_order</text>
    <rect class="node" x="515" y="95" width="100" height="45" rx="8"/>
    <text class="cap" x="565" y="123" style="font-size:12px">check_stock</text>
    <rect class="node feat" x="515" y="175" width="100" height="45" rx="8"/>
    <text class="cap" x="565" y="203" style="font-size:12px">confirm</text>
    <rect class="node hot" x="385" y="175" width="100" height="45" rx="8"/>
    <text class="cap" x="435" y="203" style="font-size:12px">human gate</text>
    <path class="edge" d="M 485 117 L 510 117"/>
    <path class="edge" d="M 565 140 L 565 170"/>
    <path class="edge" d="M 510 197 L 490 197"/>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="3">
    <rect class="zone" x="680" y="50" width="280" height="200"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="820" y="78">THE PRODUCTION LAYER</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="705" y="95" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="725" y="120" style="font-size:13px">checkpointer: thread per customer</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="705" y="145" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="725" y="170" style="font-size:13px">recursion_limit + retry policy</text>
    <rect class="card hot" x="705" y="195" width="230" height="40"/>
    <text class="cardtext" x="725" y="220" style="font-size:13px">interrupt() before the till moves</text>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment pop" data-fragment-index="4">
    <rect class="card ok" x="290" y="320" width="420" height="100"/>
    <text class="zonelabel" x="500" y="350">THE REAL WORLD</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="320" y="380" style="font-size:13px">stock: oat milk 2 -> 1 · till: $200.00 -> $206.25</text>
    <text class="cardtext" x="320" y="404" style="font-size:13px">every number asserted by an oracle, every run</text>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="4" pathLength="1" d="M 180 250 L 400 320"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="4" pathLength="1" d="M 500 250 L 500 315"/>
    <path class="edge draw fragment" data-fragment-index="4" pathLength="1" d="M 820 250 L 600 320"/>
  </g>
  <g class="fragment" data-fragment-index="5">
    <text class="cap big" x="500" y="470">Success is a property of the world, not the transcript</text>
  </g>
</svg>
```

---

## Ten questions, answered

| # | The question | Where the deck answered it |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Need an agent at all? | Do You Need an Agent at All? |
| 2 | What counts as success? | Define Done, Verifiably |
| 3 | What can it see and do? | Tools Are Round Trips |
| 4 | Which decisions stay code? | Graphs Are Data (the price node has no LLM) |
| 5 | Authoritative state? | State and Reducers · Walk Away, Come Back |
| 6 | Simplest graph that works? | Chains Can't Loop · Do You Need an Agent at All? |
| 7 | Stop, retry, escalate? | Stop, Retry, Escalate |
| 8 | Replay-safe side effects? | Resume Without Double-Charging |
| 9 | Authority and approval? | Approve Before You Act |
| 10 | What does each call buy? | Do You Need an Agent at All? (the call-count scorecard) |

::: notes
The checklist from the opening, closed. If a viewer can answer these ten
about their own system, the deck did its job; the APIs were just the
vehicle.
:::

---

## References

**Guides**
[OpenAI, A practical guide to building agents](https://openai.com/business/guides-and-resources/a-practical-guide-to-building-ai-agents/) · [Anthropic, Building effective agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents) · [Anthropic, Demystifying evals for AI agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals-for-ai-agents)

**LangChain / LangGraph docs**
[LangGraph persistence](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/persistence) · [Interrupts](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/interrupts) · [LangChain messages, runnables, tools](https://docs.langchain.com/)

**Also drawn on**
[AutoGen, termination](https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/stable/user-guide/agentchat-user-guide/tutorial/termination.html) · [12-factor agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

**Siblings**
[How Git Works, Visually](../../how-git-works/website/git.html) · [How RAG Works, Visually](../../how-rag-works/website/rag.html) · [How Agentic Harnesses Work, Visually](../../how-agentic-harnesses-work/website/harness.html)

::: {.src}
Every figure in this deck: a real run of langchain 1.3 / langgraph 1.2 at Beanline Coffee, regenerable with build/regen_all.py · versions pinned in build/requirements.txt
:::
